1.description: Timing premium Effect of horizon-dependent risk aversion (HDRA) on willingness to pay for early resolution of uncertainty (timing premium), compared to Epstein-Zin preferences (EZ) with γ = 10. Calibration of Table 1, with ρ = 1 and β=0.9989⁠.

2.description: Term structure of dividend strip expected excess returns Slope of the unconditional expected excess returns of annual dividend strip futures: logE[RnF]−logE[R5F] for all n≥2⁠. HDRA stands for “Horizon-dependent risk aversion,” Standard LRR stands for the standard “Long-run risk” model (⁠γ~=γ=10 and ρ=1/1.5 are the Bansal, Kiku, and Yaron [2012] parameters). The calibration of both models is from Table 1. Data are from Giglio, Kelly, and Kozak (Forthcoming) using dividend strip proxies (1973–2020 sample). See Internet Appendix IA.G for details.

3.description: Dividend strip future Sharpe ratios Slope of the unconditional Sharpe ratios of annual dividend strip futures: SR[RnF]−SR[R5F] for all n≥2⁠. HDRA stands for “Horizon-dependent risk aversion,” Standard LRR stands for the standard “Long-run risk” model (⁠γ~=γ=10 and ρ=1/1.5 are the Bansal, Kiku, and Yaron [2012] parameters). The calibration of both models is from Table 1. Data are from Giglio, Kelly, and Kozak (Forthcoming) using dividend strip proxies (1973–2020 sample). See Internet Appendix IA.G for details.

4.description: Term structure of forward equity yields: Normal times Slope of the forward equity yields of annual dividend strip futures: E[FEYn]−E[FEY5] for all n≥2⁠, under the one-period model. HDRA stands for “Horizon-dependent risk aversion,” Standard LRR stands for the standard “Long-run risk” model (⁠γ~=γ=10 and ρ=1/1.5 are the Bansal, Kiku, and Yaron [2012] parameters). The calibration of both models is from Table 1. Data are from Giglio, Kelly, and Kozak (Forthcoming) using dividend strip proxies (1973–2020 sample excluding NBER recessions). See Internet Appendix IA.G for details.

5.description: Term structure of forward equity yields: Recessions Slope of the forward equity yields of annual dividend strip futures: E[FEYn]−E[FEY5] for all n≥2⁠, under the buy-and-hold model. HDRA stands for “Horizon-dependent risk aversion,” Standard LRR stands for the standard “Long-run risk” model (⁠γ~=γ=10 and ρ=1/1.5 are the Bansal, Kiku, and Yaron [2012] parameters). The calibration of both models is from Table 1. Data are from Giglio, Kelly, and Kozak (Forthcoming) using dividend strip proxies (1973–2020 sample restricted to NBER recessions). See Internet Appendix IA.G for details.

B.1.description: Dividend strip future expected returns loading on index dividend-price ratio β-loadings of the regressions of dividend strip futures expected returns at horizon h≥1  logEt[RhF] on the index dividend/price ratio dt−pt⁠; under one-period trading. HDRA stands for “Horizon-dependent risk aversion,” Standard LRR stands for the standard “Long-run risk” model (⁠γ~=γ=10 and ρ=1/1.5 are the parameters of Bansal, Kiku, and Yaron 2012). The calibration of both models is from Table 1. All returns are monthly, annualized.
